---
title: "MaxDiff Analysis of MSBA Class Preferences"
subtitle: "Counts · MLE · Bayesian Estimation of the MNL Model"
author: "Fancheng Xia"
date: today
jupyter: mgta495
format:
  revealjs:
    embed-resources: true
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
    slide-number: true
    fontsize: 1.55em
    margin: 0.07
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#FFFFFF"
    css: |
      .reveal .slides .title-slide h1 { color: #2C7BB6 !important; font-weight: 700; }
      .reveal .slides .title-slide .subtitle { color: #555 !important; }
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: false
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib as mpl
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# ── Palette aligned with cosmo/blog blue ──────────────────────────────────────
BLUE   = "#2C7BB6"
TEAL   = "#1A9988"
RED    = "#D7191C"
GRAY   = "#6C757D"
LIGHT  = "#EAF3FB"
BG     = "#FFFFFF"

mpl.rcParams.update({
    "figure.facecolor": BG,
    "axes.facecolor":   BG,
    "axes.edgecolor":   "#CCCCCC",
    "axes.linewidth":   0.8,
    "axes.spines.top":  False,
    "axes.spines.right":False,
    "grid.color":       "#E5E5E5",
    "grid.linewidth":   0.6,
    "font.family":      "sans-serif",
    "font.size":        11,
    "xtick.color":      GRAY,
    "ytick.color":      "#333333",
})

df_raw = pd.read_csv("files/maxdiff_design_and_choices.csv")
labels_df = pd.read_csv("files/maxdiff_item_labels.csv", usecols=[0, 1])
labels_df.columns = ["item", "label"]
labels_df["short"] = [
    "AI Math", "SQL", "Mkt/Fin/Ops", "Large Data", "Biz Analytics",
    "Analytics Consulting", "Customer Analytics", "Capstone",
    "Mkt Analytics", "Biz Intelligence",
]
short_map = dict(zip(labels_df["item"], labels_df["short"]))

task_sizes = df_raw.groupby(["Record ID", "Task"]).size()
complete = task_sizes[task_sizes == 4].reset_index()[["Record ID", "Task"]]
df = df_raw.merge(complete, on=["Record ID", "Task"])
N = df["Record ID"].nunique()
T = df.groupby("Record ID")["Task"].nunique().iloc[0]

# ── Counts ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
item_stats = df.groupby("Item").agg(
    times_shown=("Response", "count"),
    times_best=("Response", lambda x: (x == 1).sum()),
    times_worst=("Response", lambda x: (x == -1).sum()),
).reset_index()
item_stats["pct_best"]  = item_stats["times_best"]  / item_stats["times_shown"]
item_stats["pct_worst"] = item_stats["times_worst"] / item_stats["times_shown"]
item_stats["score"]     = item_stats["pct_best"] - item_stats["pct_worst"]
item_stats["label"]     = item_stats["Item"].map(short_map)
item_stats = item_stats.sort_values("score", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# ── MNL task arrays ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
tasks_raw = []
for (rid, tid), g in df.groupby(["Record ID", "Task"]):
    items = g["Item"].values
    best  = g.loc[g["Response"] ==  1, "Item"].values[0]
    worst = g.loc[g["Response"] == -1, "Item"].values[0]
    tasks_raw.append((items, best, worst))

n_tasks = len(tasks_raw)
items_mat     = np.zeros((n_tasks, 4), dtype=int)
best_arr      = np.zeros(n_tasks, dtype=int)
worst_arr     = np.zeros(n_tasks, dtype=int)
remaining_mat = np.zeros((n_tasks, 3), dtype=int)

for t, (items, best, worst) in enumerate(tasks_raw):
    idx = np.array(items) - 1
    items_mat[t]     = idx
    best_arr[t]      = best - 1
    worst_arr[t]     = worst - 1
    remaining_mat[t] = idx[idx != (best - 1)]

# ── MLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
from scipy.optimize import minimize

def log_likelihood(beta):
    b = np.concatenate([[0.0], beta])
    u  = b[items_mat]
    m1 = u.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)
    ll_best  = b[best_arr]  - (m1.squeeze() + np.log(np.sum(np.exp(u - m1), axis=1)))
    nw = -b[remaining_mat]
    m2 = nw.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)
    ll_worst = -b[worst_arr] - (m2.squeeze() + np.log(np.sum(np.exp(nw - m2), axis=1)))
    return np.sum(ll_best) + np.sum(ll_worst)

res = minimize(lambda x: -log_likelihood(x), x0=np.zeros(9),
               method="BFGS", options={"maxiter": 10000, "gtol": 1e-6})
beta_mle  = np.concatenate([[0.0], res.x])
shares_mle = np.exp(beta_mle) / np.exp(beta_mle).sum()
mle_df = pd.DataFrame({"Item": range(1, 11),
                        "Label": [short_map[i] for i in range(1, 11)],
                        "share": shares_mle}
                      ).sort_values("share", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# ── Bayesian MCMC ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def log_prior(beta): return np.sum(-0.5 * beta**2 / 10.0)
def log_post(beta):  return log_likelihood(beta) + log_prior(beta)

np.random.seed(42)
n_iter, step, K = 20000, 0.08, 9
beta, lp = np.zeros(K), log_post(np.zeros(K))
draws, accept = np.zeros((n_iter, K)), 0
for t in range(n_iter):
    prop = beta + np.random.normal(0, step, K)
    lp_p = log_post(prop)
    if np.log(np.random.uniform()) < lp_p - lp:
        beta, lp, accept = prop, lp_p, accept + 1
    draws[t] = beta

post = draws[n_iter // 4:]
bf   = np.column_stack([np.zeros(len(post)), post])
sd   = np.exp(bf) / np.exp(bf).sum(axis=1, keepdims=True)
shares_post  = sd.mean(axis=0)
shares_ci_lo = np.percentile(sd, 2.5,  axis=0)
shares_ci_hi = np.percentile(sd, 97.5, axis=0)

bayes_df = pd.DataFrame({"Item": range(1, 11),
                          "Label": [short_map[i] for i in range(1, 11)],
                          "share": shares_post,
                          "lo": shares_ci_lo,
                          "hi": shares_ci_hi}
                        ).sort_values("share", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
```

## What is MaxDiff? {background-color="#EAF3FB"}

**The problem with rating scales**

- Respondents use Likert scales inconsistently — one person's "7" is another's "9"
- No forced differentiation: everything ends up rated highly

**MaxDiff (Best-Worst Scaling)**

- Each screen: see **4 items**, pick **most** and **least** preferred
- Forces real trade-offs → more reliable, comparable preferences

**This study:** 10 MSBA core courses · `{python} int(N)` respondents · `{python} int(T)` tasks each

---

## Three Methods, One Question {background-color="#EAF3FB"}

> *Which MSBA courses do students prefer most?*

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%"}
**① Counts**

$$\text{score}_j = \%\text{best}_j - \%\text{worst}_j$$

Fast · transparent · zero modeling assumptions
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**② MNL via MLE**

$$P(\text{best}=j) = \frac{e^{\beta_j}}{\sum_k e^{\beta_k}}$$

Accounts for competitive context · standard errors
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**③ MNL via Bayes**

$$\pi(\beta \mid y) \propto L(\beta)\,\pi(\beta)$$

Full posterior → uncertainty on derived quantities
:::

::::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
Each method adds something the previous one lacks.
:::

---

## Results: All Three Methods Agree

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
counts_cmp = item_stats[["Item","label","score"]].copy()
counts_cmp.columns = ["Item","Label","cs"]
mle_cmp   = mle_df[["Item","share"]].rename(columns={"share":"ms"})
bayes_cmp = bayes_df[["Item","share","lo","hi"]].rename(columns={"share":"bs"})
cmp = counts_cmp.merge(mle_cmp, on="Item").merge(bayes_cmp, on="Item")
cmp = cmp.sort_values("ms").reset_index(drop=True)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(13, 4.4))

def bar_color(vals, threshold=0):
    return [BLUE if v > threshold else RED for v in vals]

# ① Counts
ax = axes[0]
ax.barh(range(10), cmp["cs"], color=bar_color(cmp["cs"]), alpha=0.88, height=0.65)
ax.set_yticks(range(10)); ax.set_yticklabels(cmp["Label"], fontsize=9)
ax.axvline(0, color="#999", lw=0.8, ls="--")
ax.set_title("① Counts Score", fontsize=11, color="#1A1A2E", pad=8)
ax.set_xlabel("% Best − % Worst", fontsize=9, color=GRAY)

# ② MLE
ax = axes[1]
ax.barh(range(10), cmp["ms"], color=bar_color(cmp["ms"], 0.10), alpha=0.88, height=0.65)
ax.set_yticks(range(10)); ax.set_yticklabels(cmp["Label"], fontsize=9)
ax.axvline(0.10, color="#999", lw=0.8, ls="--")
ax.set_title("② MLE Share", fontsize=11, color="#1A1A2E", pad=8)
ax.set_xlabel("Preference Share", fontsize=9, color=GRAY)

# ③ Bayes
ax = axes[2]
ax.barh(range(10), cmp["bs"], color=bar_color(cmp["bs"], 0.10), alpha=0.88, height=0.65)
ax.errorbar(cmp["bs"], range(10),
            xerr=[cmp["bs"]-cmp["lo"], cmp["hi"]-cmp["bs"]],
            fmt="none", color="#333", capsize=3, lw=1.1)
ax.set_yticks(range(10)); ax.set_yticklabels(cmp["Label"], fontsize=9)
ax.axvline(0.10, color="#999", lw=0.8, ls="--")
ax.set_title("③ Bayesian Posterior Share", fontsize=11, color="#1A1A2E", pad=8)
ax.set_xlabel("Posterior Mean Share + 95% CI", fontsize=9, color=GRAY)

fig.suptitle("Three-Method Comparison — MSBA Class Preferences",
             fontsize=12, color="#1A1A2E", y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

::: {.fragment}
**Mkt Analytics (#9) dominates all three.** · Middle cluster rankings vary — that's where real uncertainty lives.
:::

---

## When to Use Each Method {background-color="#EAF3FB"}

| Method | Best for | Key limitation |
|--------|----------|----------------|
| **Counts** | Quick first pass, executive summary | Ignores competitive context |
| **MLE** | Point estimate + standard errors | CIs on derived quantities need delta method |
| **Bayes** | Uncertainty on shares, downstream decisions | Computationally heavier |

. . .

**The Bayesian advantage in one sentence:**

> Push every MCMC draw through softmax → credible intervals on preference shares, *for free* — no delta method required.

. . .

**Next step →** Hierarchical Bayes: individual-level $\beta_i$ to find preference *segments* (tech vs. finance vs. consulting track).
